base.name: Purdue University

englishRequirement.note: English test (TOEFL 80+, IELTS 6.5+, or Duolingo 105+) required for international students.

base.notes: All engineering programs require minimum 3.0 GPA. The following courses must be COMPLETED (not in-progress) at the time of application: Calculus I, Calculus II, Calculus-based Chemistry I, Calculus-based Physics I, and one Advanced Science/Math/Physics course. ENGR 13100 and ENGR 13200 are recommended to reach 24 credits but are NOT required (except for IDES). Transfer applicants from outside articulation pathway programs are reviewed on a space-available basis. Source: admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/transfer/engineering-transfer-criteria/

majors.name: Aeronautical & Astronautical Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only. Outside transfer applicants are not admitted directly.

majors.name: Biomedical Engineering
majors.note: The required Advanced Science/Math/Physics course must be equivalent to one of: Chemistry II (CHM 11610/11620/11630), CS 15900, CS 18000, or CS 17600.

majors.name: Chemical Engineering
majors.note: The required Advanced Science/Math/Physics course MUST be Chemistry II (equivalent to CHM 11610/11620/11630). Other science/math courses are not accepted for this major.

majors.name: Computer Engineering
majors.note: Chemistry I is NOT required — another physics or math course may be substituted. The Advanced Science course may be another math or physics course.

majors.name: Electrical Engineering
majors.note: Chemistry I is NOT required — another physics or math course may be substituted. The Advanced Science course may be another math or physics course.

majors.name: Environmental & Ecological Engineering
majors.note: The required Advanced Science/Math/Physics course MUST be Chemistry II (equivalent to CHM 11610/11620/11630). Other science/math courses are not accepted for this major.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED at West Lafayette — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only. The Indianapolis campus is open to all qualified transfer applicants.

majors.name: Motorsports Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only.

majors.name: Interdisciplinary Engineering (IDES)
majors.note: ENGR 13100 and ENGR 13200 (Transforming Ideas to Innovations I & II) are specifically required for IDES and cannot be waived. Must also submit a one-page statement of interest.
